_purpose: Publication-readiness gate checklist for the Reality Audit public anisotropy program.
_note: Each gate item defines a required condition, severity (required / recommended), and a short description. The gate evaluator checks run metadata and comparison summaries against these criteria.

gates.description: A preregistration plan is recorded in run metadata for any result labeled confirmatory.
gates.detail: Exploratory runs may proceed without a preregistration plan, but results cannot be called confirmatory without one.

gates.description: The preregistration plan must have _locked: true for a confirmatory result.
gates.detail: An unlocked plan is not a binding commitment and cannot prevent post-hoc specification.

gates.description: Each catalog must use a null model appropriate for its instrument.
gates.detail: Wide-field gamma-ray detectors (Fermi, Swift) use exposure_corrected null. Neutrino detectors (IceCube) use isotropic null.

gates.description: Multiple-testing correction is applied and is recorded in run metadata.
gates.detail: Acceptable methods: holm, bonferroni. Method 'none' is flagged as insufficient.

gates.description: At least two independent catalogs are present in the comparison.
gates.detail: A single-catalog result is hypothesis-generating only. Replication across instruments is required.

gates.description: The comparison output must include an explicit replication verdict.
gates.detail: Acceptable verdicts: both_anomaly, partial_replication, inconsistent, neither_anomaly.

gates.description: Known systematics and caveats must be documented in the comparison memo.
gates.detail: At minimum: instrument acceptance correction is an approximation; small-N catalogs are underpowered.

gates.description: All required output artifacts (summary JSON, memo markdown, comparison JSON) must be present.
gates.detail: Missing artifacts mean the run was not fully completed or outputs were deleted.

gates.description: A cross-catalog comparison memo is present in the output directory.
gates.detail: The memo is the primary internal scientific record of the cross-catalog comparison.

gates.description: Run memos must not make unsupported metaphysical claims.
gates.detail: Statistical anisotropy is not evidence for consciousness, intentionality, or non-physical mechanisms. Any such language is a disqualifying error.

gates.description: External scientific review has not yet been completed.
gates.detail: This is a status flag, not a gate blocker. Tracks whether external review has been filed.

gates.description: IceCube HESE small-N limitation is acknowledged in any result using that catalog.
gates.detail: IceCube HESE has ~37 events; the result is exploratory regardless of percentile.

gates.description: Each catalog has an explicit exposure quality tier recorded (even if 'none').
gates.detail: Exposure quality tier documents the provenance and limitations of the exposure model used. Any tier — including 'none' — is acceptable as long as it is explicitly documented.

readiness_levels.not_ready: Required gates are failing. Do not circulate this result.
readiness_levels.internally_reviewable: All required gates pass. Suitable for internal team review only.
readiness_levels.candidate_first_results_note: All required and recommended gates pass. Suitable for a candidate internal first-results note.
readiness_levels.ready_for_external_review: All gates pass and external review has been initiated.
